findings.title: Unpinned External Package Installation (Supply Chain Risk)
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to install and execute packages from external sources (npm, PyPI) without specifying version pins or integrity checks. This exposes the user to potential supply chain attacks if those packages are compromised, and the agent may execute arbitrary code from the external source without adequate vetting.
findings.evidence: Instructions contain: 'npx @anthropic-workspace/standards-cli init' and 'pip install workspace-standards-toolkit'. Both commands install unpinned packages from public registries.
findings.remediation: Pin package versions (e.g., '@anthropic-workspace/standards-cli@1.2.3') and verify package integrity (e.g., checksums signatures). Consider using a package lock file or internal mirror to reduce supply chain risk.
